mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate above 4.2% signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 4.2% threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during disinflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 205.0 indicates persistent price pressures that force equity investors to discount future cash flows more heavily, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 205.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for Fed policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue expansion, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals unsustainable demand that precedes earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The divergence metric produces positive forward returns in greater than 55% of instances, or the interaction term loses predictive power when GDP data is revised.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below -0.5% signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks that disrupt manufacturing output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines below -0.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of stable inventory levels.
